expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: search box selector dropdown planning covers OK or Cancel confirmation, pending selection, and dismissal outcomes
expectations.evidence: In result.md under “Phase 4a checkpoint intent (what this phase must plan for)”, it explicitly lists: “1. OK confirmation outcome 2. Cancel confirmation outcome 3. Pending selection state … 4. Dismissal outcomes … and what happens to pending selection”. The subsequent checklist sections A–D also cover OK, Cancel, pending selection, and dismissal tests.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md is titled “QA Plan Orchestrator — Phase 4a (Confirmation & Dismissal Outcomes)” and states “Primary phase under test: phase4a”. The content is scoped to phase4a confirmation/dismissal planning (OK/Cancel/pending/dismissal) and avoids asserting product-specific behavior due to “blind_pre_defect” evidence constraints.
